workflow: Marble fractured gangsaw bench: cost-wise vs volume-wise yield optimisation (max NET value, balanced, max VOLUME)
fracture_model: 3 oblique planes x = a + by*y + bz*z; one cell (0.5 m) keep-out at each crossing
objective: maximise  net_usd + W * volume_m3 ; W is a volume credit ($/m3) that sweeps the Pareto front
recommendation: Cost is the more important figure (brief). BALANCED is the recommended operating point: it recovers 5.0 m3 more stone than the pure-profit cut for only $1000 (3.9%) of net, by filling the break-even 1.0 m pockets the cost-optimal cut leaves empty. Pushing to MAX-VOLUME adds only 2.5 m3 more but burns $7950 of profit, because it must restructure premium gangsaw runs into loss-making 1x1x1 offcuts.

guillotine_cuts_balanced.sequence: 1) two full-span cuts perpendicular to Y -> 3 slices; 2) two full-span cuts perpendicular to Z -> 9 beams (1x1x6 m); 3) 25 rip cuts perpendicular to X within each beam at the block boundaries (edge-to-edge inside the beam).
